title: Bruins’ Charlie McAvoy scores winning goal. And then it was time to see the dentist — again.
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Charlie McAvoy scored the game-winning goal in overtime to lead the Bruins to a 2-1 victory over the Kings.
McAvoy played despite a mouth injury from a previous game, and returned to play after being hit into the glass.
The Bruins have no captain currently; McAvoy's resilience is recognized as he serves as an alternate captain.

title: Jaylen Brown blasts refs on social media after being ejected from Spurs game
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown was ejected in the second quarter of the Celtics' loss to the Spurs after receiving two technical fouls.
Brown criticized referees on social media, echoing past complaints about poor officiating in previous games against the Spurs.
The Celtics, missing Payton Pritchard and Nikola Vucevic, fell to the Spurs 125-116, with Brown's absence impacting the team.

title: Man sentenced for fatally stabbing Chelsea woman, 59
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Angel Alvarez, 68, was sentenced to life in prison for the murder of Margarita Morehead, with parole possible after 17 years.
Alvarez pleaded guilty to second-degree murder, violating a protection order, and assault with a dangerous weapon in Chelsea, MA.
Victim Margarita Morehead was fatally stabbed by Alvarez after an argument; she had previously obtained a restraining order against him.

title: MBTA police seeking suspect who allegedly spat on, hit rider
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: The alleged assault occurred on February 27 at around 8 p.m. in Prudential station, according to MBTA Transit Police.
The suspect is accused of spitting on and striking a Green Line rider multiple times with a closed fist.
Transit Police released images of the suspect and urge anyone with information to contact their criminal investigations unit.

title: Patriots reportedly remain interested in A.J. Brown trade after signing Romeo Doubs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: New England Patriots signed Romeo Doubs to a four-year contract worth up to $80 million.
Patriots still interested in Eagles' receiver A.J. Brown despite signing Doubs to strengthen their wide receiver position.
Eagles demand a 2027 first-round pick and 2026 second-round pick for Brown, whose trade could be delayed due to cap implications.

title: She’s running the Boston Marathon to support breast cancer patients
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Sydney Weisman, 24, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support the Ellie Fund, aiding breast cancer patients.
The Ellie Fund provides transportation, meals, childcare, and daily support for individuals and families affected by breast cancer.
Weisman draws inspiration from her mother's breast cancer journey, where she was among the first to try cold capping therapy.

title: 3 Stop & Shop locations in Boston, Reading receive bomb threats, police say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Three Stop & Shop locations in Boston and Reading received bomb threats on Monday afternoon.
Bomb squads found no credible threats at Jamaica Plain, Allston, and Reading stores after thorough searches.
Police are investigating the possibility of the threats being related, with no suspect identified yet.

title: 4 things to know about new Patriots WR Romeo Doubs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots sign WR Romeo Doubs to a four-year, $68 million contract, with a max value of $80 million.
Doubs recorded 55 catches for 724 yards and six touchdowns with the Packers in the 2025 season.
He excelled in the playoffs, with 20 catches for 371 yards and two touchdowns across four career games.

title: Southbridge woman dies after being rescued from house fire
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A woman in her 60s died after being rescued from a house fire in Southbridge on Monday.
The fire, which began in the kitchen, had no audible fire alarms, marking the eighth smoke alarm-related death in Massachusetts this year.
The woman was first taken to UMass Memorial-Harington Hospital, then to Mass General, where she passed away, officials confirmed.

title: New ‘call a boomer’ phone line connects young people in Boston to seniors in Nevada
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Matter Neuroscience launched a 'call a boomer' phone line connecting Boston youth with Reno seniors to bridge generational gaps.
The phone line allows free calls between Boston's Commonwealth Avenue and Sierra Manor in Reno, promoting intergenerational conversations.
Previous installations in San Francisco and Abilene facilitated over 250 conversations, aiming to reduce political and age-related divisions.
